reasoning: While the LLM fidelity scores indicate near-perfect content preservation, programmatic detectors identified a critical pipeline failure where header/footer artifacts were retained across all 5 pages of the document. Additionally, there is a major structural defect involving flattened mixed-number lists. Per the strict count-based routing rules, the presence of a critical programmatic defect requires automatic rejection.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 5; Page 3 of 5
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source PDF contains numerous list items on pages 3, 4, and 5 where the introductory phrase is both bold and underlined (e.g., 'Agriculture', 'Air Quality', 'Costs'). The extraction completely loses this emphasis, providing the plain text without any markdown for bolding (** text **) or any mechanism to preserve the underline.

action_items: Investigate pipeline failure causing header/footer retention
Fix list flattening issue with mixed alphanumeric/roman bulleting
Restore bolding/underline markdown on list introductory phrases
